paper_title: A Simple Theory of Economic Development at the Extensive Industry Margin
research_answer: On average, countries are missing about 8–9% of the occupational employment required to enter the industries they don't yet export. Specifically, measuring distance using wage-bill shares gives an average distance of 0.079, while using employment shares yields 0.092. However, the variation around this average is substantial — the maximum distance reaches 0.718 under the wage-bill measure and 0.772 under the employment measure, meaning some countries are missing more than 70% of the qualifications needed for their potential new industries. This wide spread confirms that while the average country is only modestly capability-constrained in aggregate, some countries face dramatically higher barriers to expanding their export baskets.
supporting_quotes: The average distance μ indicates that about 8%-9% of qualified employment is missing.
There is, however, quite some variation, with a maximum distance of over 70% for both indicators.
